title: DistributionDraft
description: An approval-first distribution draft produced by the Product Distribution OS. A draft is never an external send: it is internal content (L1 evidence) that a human must review before any manual copy/send. Channels are manual-only by design; no automation, no cold outreach, no scraping.

prospect_id.description: Reference to the prospect in data/distribution/prospects.json.

sector.description: Target sector used to shape the offer angle.

channel.description: Manual-only channel. There is no automation behind any value here.

subject.description: Optional subject line (used for email/proposal channels).

body.description: Draft body. Must pass auto_client_acquisition.governance_os.policy_check_draft and carry no PII.

offer_angle.description: The value angle the draft leads with.

evidence_level.description: Maps to auto_client_acquisition.proof_engine.evidence.EvidenceLevel. A generated draft is L1 (internal draft, not customer-ready) until a human reviews it.

approval_required.description: Always true. No draft leaves the queue without founder approval.

status.description: Lifecycle state. Generators only ever emit 'pending_approval' or 'needs_edit'. Human/approval flow sets the rest. 'sent_via_integration' is only valid AFTER approval and is never written by any script in this layer.

policy_issues.description: Issues raised by policy_check_draft / PII scan. Non-empty implies status must be needs_edit.

next_action.description: Human-readable next step for the founder.
